tasks.name: AINews_Mine
prompts:
Today is {longdate}. Your goal: save qualifying arXiv cs.AI papers to files for later analysis. Follow these steps EXACTLY.

STEP 1 - Fetch listing.
  a) Call fetch_page_text(url="https://r.jina.ai/https://arxiv.org/list/cs.AI/new", max_words=2500) - NO query parameter.
  b) The result will be truncated. Find the key in the truncation notice ("auto-saved to scratchpad key: _tc_r..."). Call scratch_load with that key to get the complete listing.

STEP 2 - Extract candidates. From the FULL listing, note the arXiv ID (format: 2603.12345) and title of up to 10 papers.

STEP 3 - Process each candidate. For each of the 10 candidates:
  a) Call fetch_page_text(url="https://r.jina.ai/https://arxiv.org/abs/<ID>", max_words=1000) - NO query parameter.
  b) From the truncated content shown in the tool result (do NOT call scratch_load), decide KEEP or DISCARD.
     KEEP: new model, training technique, benchmark, dataset, or novel method with measurable results.
     DISCARD: survey, position paper, or no concrete technical contribution.
  c) If KEEP: call write_from_scratch(scratch_key="<key from truncation notice>", path="webresearch/01-Mine/{today}/NNN-<slug>.md")
     (NNN = zero-padded counter from 001; slug = 3-5 hyphenated words from title)
  d) If DISCARD: move to the next candidate.

Stop after writing 8 files or processing all 10 candidates. When done, reply with a brief list of the files written. Do NOT call web_search or scratch_load for abstract pages.

tasks.name: AINews_Mine_Test
prompts:
Today is {longdate}. The goal is to find new AI News as save for later analysis.
STEP 1 - Find links: Search the internet for scientific papers, company announcements, and reports of new architectures. Save the links to an ai_headlines.json file of headlines and URLs.

tasks.name: AINews_Analyse
prompts: Today is {longdate}. This is an analysis task - all source material is already in webresearch/01-Mine/{today}/. Read every markdown file there. Synthesize a developer-focused AI daily newsletter with 6 to 8 items. Deduplicate: if two articles cover the same paper or topic, merge them into one entry. For each item write approximately 400 words covering exactly three sub-sections: (1) What it is - the technical contribution explained precisely, with key architecture choices, metrics, or results; (2) Why it matters - its significance to the broader research landscape; (3) How to apply it - concrete ways an AI developer could use this work today, e.g. a technique to try, a dataset to pull, a library to evaluate, or a line of research to follow. Order items by practical developer relevance (most actionable first). Open with a 2-3 sentence executive summary of the day's theme. Save the complete newsletter to webresearch/02-Analysis/{today}/ai_daily.md.

tasks.name: AINews_Present
prompts: Convert the AI news analysis at webresearch/02-Analysis/{today}/ai_daily.md to a polished HTML file. Use the dark angular template at controldata/html_template/tech_company_website_black_angular.jsx as the visual reference for style: dark background, angular geometric accents, monospace headings, high-contrast text. Each newsletter item should be a self-contained card section. Include a page header with the date {longdate} and a subtitle 'AI Research Daily - Developer Edition'. Save the output to webresearch/03-Presentation/{today}/ai_daily.html.
